questions.question: Syntax is defined in Lesson 3 as what?

choices.B: The form or structure of expressions, statements, and program units

questions.question: Semantics is defined as what?

choices.A: The set of sentences in a language
choices.C: The meaning of expressions, statements, and program units
choices.D: The set of terminals and nonterminals

questions.question: Which group is listed as users of a language definition?
A lexeme is defined as what?

choices.D: The lowest level syntactic unit of a language

questions.question: A token is defined as what?

choices.D: A sentence with only terminal symbols

questions.question: In formal definitions of languages, what is a recognizer?

choices.C: A device that reads input strings and decides if they belong to the language
choices.D: A list of tokens and lexemes

questions.question: Context-free grammars were developed by whom and when?
Backus-Naur Form (BNF) was invented by whom to describe the syntax of Algol 58?
In BNF, a rule has a left-hand side (LHS) and a right-hand side (RHS). The LHS is what?
What is a start symbol in a grammar?

choices.C: A special element of the nonterminals of a grammar

questions.question: In a derivation, a sentence is a sentential form that has only what?
A leftmost derivation is one in which which nonterminal is expanded each time?
A grammar is ambiguous if it generates a sentential form that has how many distinct parse trees?
In EBNF, optional parts are placed in which symbols?
In EBNF, repetitions (0 or more) are placed in which symbols?
Static semantics has nothing to do with what?
Which is an example of a non-context-free constraint listed in Lesson 3?

choices.B: Variables must be declared before they are used

questions.question: Operational semantics describes meaning by doing what?

choices.A: Executing statements on a machine and observing state changes

questions.question: Denotational semantics is based on what?
Axiomatic semantics is based on what?
